blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What was she talking about now?
From Araboth to here, there was a barrier and the distance was immense.
They don't know what Ikael's eyesight was, but there was not the slightest chance that she was looking at this place.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Don't be so disappointed. For angels, 'seeing' is different from how humans 'see.' There are many perspectives on 'seeing,' and among them, 'seeing' is the ability to see what you want to see. In short, angels can see anything they want if they desire it.
At first, they couldn't imagine it well.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: According to Peope, if they wanted to see something, they could just see it.
Even a monster with a hundred eyes from a fairy tale wouldn't harass a prince with such an absurd ability.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: It's true. The reason Ikasa arrived first after the Elixir of Life broke was because she was overlooking Shamain. Well... she's still a fallen angel because she's so petty.
Since the fallen angels living in the Second Heaven are lower than the fairies living in the Fourth Heaven, Peope criticized Ikasa without hesitation.
It showed just how far a top-ranking angel could fall when they committed a sin.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: But if angels can see whatever they want, doesn't that mean they know everything in the world?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Not necessarily. Deciding what to see is difficult. Think about it. Even if you could overlook, could you grasp everything in the world?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: No. It would be difficult.
Shirone recalled the time an angel visited Nors Shelter.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Godlock had said that an angel could find this place if they wanted, but it was unlikely to happen.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: No one checks their fingerprints every day.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Oh? You knew that story? Exactly. Anyway, if Archangel Ikael-nim is overlooking this place, she might respond to my voice.
Canis was irritated. As a mage, he disliked relying on probabilities, and this case was especially extreme.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: The odds are slim. This place could be her fingerprint. And even if she is overlooking, what's the guarantee she'll help us?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: There is no guarantee. I came along to offer even a sliver of hope. Waiting for us in Zebul is Archangel Kariel. Do you understand? You can't defeat him even if you were reborn.
Shirone faced reality. If the one holding her friends was an archangel, they needed at least an ally of the same rank to have any chance of escape.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Alright. Let's try it your way. I'll trust you.
Anyway, Peope clicked her tongue, thinking that the Nors were inherently doubtful.
After all, she saw some potential in this and jumped at it.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: No angel checks their fingerprints daily, but with Ikael, it might be different. She was said to be compassionate and often watched over the divine subjects.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then I'll begin. Once the conversation starts, don't interrupt. I don't want to commit sacrilege against the archangel.
Peope closed her eyes and concentrated. Her plea spread out in concentric circles.
Shirone waited anxiously. But after five minutes, there was no significant change in Peope.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Was it impossible after all?
The fear of losing hope of rescuing his friends was much greater than the disappointment of failure.
As the group's hearts were sinking into despair, Peope's body trembled.
A moment later, hot tears streamed from her closed eyes.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Aah, Archangel Ikael-nim.
Shirone looked back at Peope, almost breaking his head.
It was clear that the conversation with Ikael had begun. Remembering Peope's plea not to interrupt, he barely held back all the questions bursting inside him.
Peope and Ikael talked for quite a long time. She nodded repeatedly and sometimes even answered aloud.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Yes, I understand.
Peope smiled and kept her eyes closed for a long time. Still overwhelmed by the emotions, she suddenly opened her eyes and exclaimed.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Did you hear that? Did you hear it? She accepted my conversation! She accepted it!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Of course, I didn't hear it. What did she say?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Aah, Archangel Ikael-nim… She was watching over me!
Seeing Peope looking up at the sky with moist eyes, Shirone couldn't dare to ask.
He waited until Peope was ready to talk after she let her emotions settle.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, did she agree to release our friends?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: No. Archangel Ikael-nim said that because she committed a sin, she can't take a single step out of her room.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What? So we failed after all?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: But she said she would help us. We will be able to enter Araboth. But there's one condition...

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What is it? Spit it out, we don't have time.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: She said she wants to meet a boy named Shirone… alone. But who is this Shirone?
Canis and Rian looked at Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: So his name was Shirone.
Until a moment ago, Shirone was just a collection of countless concepts to Peope's eyes.
It was a vague and abstract feeling, like trying to grasp an intangible mist.
But now it was different.
Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The moment Peope thought of those three syllables, Shirone's actions, thoughts, and emotions integrated into her mind.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Only Shirone should come? Is there a particular reason?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: I don't know about that. I wanted to ask, but for some reason I don't think she wants to reveal anything.
Canis thought it was actually a good thing. To use their limited time efficiently, it was more effective to focus on their individual tasks rather than moving as a group.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Then Shirone should go meet Ikael. I don't think it will be possible for her to escape without his help anyway.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: What about you guys?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: We'll head to Zebul. Going from the Seventh Heaven to the Sixth Heaven should be a free pass. We'll go to where Amy is first. If necessary, we can buy some time.
It was the best strategy available in the current situation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Okay. You guys be careful. I'll come as soon as I can.
Shirone entered the spire without even having time to watch his friends heading to Zebul.
Inside, a vast space without even a pillar spread out. The floor was made of glass and there wasn't a single speck of dust, let alone footprints.
Peope, relying on the memories passed down from Ikirin, led Shirone to a corner of the tower.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Why isn't there anything here?
According to the memory, there should have been an elevator at this spot. But there was no visible device around.
At that moment, light leaked from a crack in the floor and a blinding light burst out.
Shirone looked back at the scenery that had suddenly changed.
They had arrived in a place where the corridor stretched out like a fish's spine.
At ten-meter intervals, there were paths on both sides, and at the end of the corridor stood a golden iron door.
Peope turned to Shirone in front of the iron door.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: From here on, it's Archangel Ikael-nim's domain. As promised, I can't go any further. Since angels can communicate with all beings, you won't need me. Just in case, I'll send you the map of Zebul.
